system.description:
Discipline: None
Skill: Grapple against Parry
Type: Normal
Qi: 9

You unfurl your sleeve or a similar strip of cloth, wrapping around your foe’s arms, legs or body, causing them to freeze with an icy coating. On a success you can launch strips of fabric at your foes, by up to 10 feet per Rank of Waijia (provided you have enough cloth to do so), and can use this to strike one foe per Rank of Waijia (or one person per 10 feet). Those affected begin to freeze starting with where they were struck. Each round they take an increasing -1d10 to their Speed, when they reach zero they are frozen completely. This coats their entire body in ice and paralyzes them. On a Total Success the duration doubles. This works against anyone from Qi Rank 1-24, regardless of your own Qi Rank. For duration, this lasts 10 minutes if the target has fewer Qi Ranks than you, but only 1d10 rounds against Targets with more Qi Ranks than you. However if your target has half as many Qi Ranks as you or less, the frozen state lasts for one day per your Rank of Qi. When this is the case they get one Endurance roll a day to break from the ice (the TN is your Resolve Rating). Characters whose Qi Rank is greater than your own can make an Endurance roll every round to break free. All other characters may make a roll every minute. Note: Due to their resistance to cold and fire Attacks, this does not work on Immortals.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
system.targetEffects: Freezing begins; Speed penalty increases by -1d10 each round until Speed reaches 0, then target is frozen and paralyzed
system.targetEffectsNote: Duration and escape cadence depend on relative Qi Rank; see entry.
